openmeter/productcatalog/addon.purpose: Domain package for add-on lifecycle management: defines the Addon aggregate (with RateCards), typed errors, domain events, and the Service + Repository interfaces. Primary constraint: domain types always carry both productcatalog.* base types and managed identity fields (NamespacedID, ManagedModel).

patterns.name: Addon aggregate embedding pattern
patterns.description: Addon embeds models.NamespacedID, models.ManagedModel, productcatalog.AddonMeta, and RateCards. RateCard wraps productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields (AddonID field). Never use struct literals missing these fields.
patterns.example: type Addon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.AddonMeta; RateCards RateCards }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: Always return addon.NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}) from adapters. Never surface raw entdb.IsNotFound. Use IsNotFound() for errors.As detection at service boundaries.
patterns.example: return nil, addon.NewNotFoundError(addon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, ID: id})

patterns.name: Domain events implement EventName / EventMetadata / Validate
patterns.description: Each lifecycle event (Create, Update, Delete, Publish, Archive) is a struct with Addon *Addon + UserID *string sourced from session.GetSessionUserID(ctx). EventName uses metadata.GetEventName with subsystem='addon', name, version='v1'.
patterns.example: func (e AddonCreateEvent) EventName() string { return metadata.GetEventName(metadata.EventType{Subsystem: AddonEventSubsystem, Name: AddonCreateEventName, Version: "v1"}) }

patterns.name: inputOptions.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues for draft validation
patterns.description: CreateAddonInput and UpdateAddonInput embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues bool}. Validate() calls models.AsValidationIssues and filters with WithSeverityOrHigher(ErrorSeverityCritical) when set. Allows draft addons with non-critical issues.
patterns.example: issues, err := models.AsValidationIssues(errors.Join(errs...)); if i.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues { issues = issues.WithSeverityOrHigher(models.ErrorSeverityCritical) }

patterns.name: RateCards.AsProductCatalogRateCards() for cross-type conversion
patterns.description: Never directly cast addon.RateCards to productcatalog.RateCards. Always call RateCards.AsProductCatalogRateCards() which iterates and extracts the inner productcatalog.RateCard from each RateCard wrapper.
patterns.example: func (c RateCards) AsProductCatalogRateCards() productcatalog.RateCards { var rcs productcatalog.RateCards; for _, rc := range c { rcs = append(rcs, rc.RateCard) }; return rcs }

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[Addon] for status/deletion guards
patterns.description: validators.go exposes IsAddonDeleted and HasAddonStatus as models.ValidatorFunc[Addon] closures. Compose them via Addon.ValidateWith() instead of inline status checks in service methods.
patterns.example: if err := addon.ValidateWith(addon.HasAddonStatus(productcatalog.AddonStatusDraft)); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: RateCard custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON on type discriminator
patterns.description: RateCard.UnmarshalJSON reads productcatalog.RateCardSerde.Type first, then switches on FlatFeeRateCardType vs UsageBasedRateCardType to pick the concrete struct. Any new rate card type requires a new case here.
patterns.example: switch s.Type { case productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCardType: serde.RateCard = &productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCard{} }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (ListAddons, CreateAddon, DeleteAddon, GetAddon, UpdateAddon, PublishAddon, ArchiveAddon, NextAddon) and all input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishAddonInput.Validate() uses a 30s jitter via clock.Now() for EffectiveFrom — do not substitute time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Addon aggregate type with Validate(), ValidateWith(), and AsProductCatalogAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plans field is *[]Plan — only populated on expand; never assume non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wraps productcatalog.RateCard with RateCardManagedFields; custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON dispatches on RateCardSerde.Type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rate card types require a case in UnmarshalJSON switch and the ratecard test.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError; IsNotFound() for errors.As detection.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never return raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap via NewNotFoundError.

key_file_guides.role: All five lifecycle event structs; UserID sourced from session.GetSessionUserID(ctx).
key_file_guides.watch_for: AddonDeleteEvent.Validate() asserts Addon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must be applied before constructing the event.

key_file_guides.role: Repository interface extends entutils.TxCreator — mandatory for TransactingRepo in the adapter layer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TxCreator embedding is required; adapters without it cannot participate in ctx-bound transactions.

key_file_guides.role: IsAddonDeleted and HasAddonStatus validator funcs for use in service-layer guards.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use via Addon.ValidateWith() instead of inline status checks.

anti_patterns: Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound errors — always wrap in addon.NewNotFoundError.
Using context.Background() in service or adapter code — always propagate ctx.
Calling RateCards adapter operations without eager-loading related rate cards (WithRatecards).
Publishing domain events outside a transaction closure — event failure should roll back the DB write.
Creating multiple non-deleted draft versions for the same key without checking HasDraft().

decisions.decision: RateCard is a separate managed type wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with AddonID
decisions.rationale: Each rate card needs its own DB identity (NamespacedID) and foreign-key reference (AddonID) separate from the base productcatalog.RateCard so Ent can persist and eager-load them independently.

decisions.decision: inputOptions.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues enables partial validation for draft addons
decisions.rationale: Draft addons can be created with non-critical issues (e.g. missing features not yet registered); callers can opt into relaxed validation that only fails on ErrorSeverityCritical.

decisions.decision: Domain events carry the full Addon pointer + UserID from session context
decisions.rationale: Downstream Watermill consumers need the full entity for side-effects; UserID supports audit trails without requiring a separate lookup.

code_examples.scenario: Creating and publishing a domain event after a successful adapter write inside a transaction

openmeter/productcatalog/plan.purpose: Domain package for plan lifecycle management: defines Plan (with Phases and RateCards), Phase/RateCard managed types, typed errors, domain events, a custom JSON serializer, and the Service + Repository interfaces. EffectivePeriod changes are only allowed via Publish/Archive, never via UpdatePlan.

patterns.name: Plan embeds PlanMeta + []Phase; Phase embeds PhaseManagedFields + productcatalog.Phase
patterns.description: Plan = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanMeta + []Phase. Phase = PhaseManagedFields{ManagedModel, NamespacedID, PlanID} + productcatalog.Phase. RateCard = productcatalog.RateCard + RateCardManagedFields{PhaseID}.
patterns.example: type Plan struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanMeta; Phases []Phase }

patterns.name: EffectivePeriod zeroed in UpdatePlanInput to prevent direct status manipulation
patterns.description: UpdatePlanInput embeds productcatalog.EffectivePeriod but the service must zero it before calling the adapter. Callers cannot set status via update; only Publish/Archive are valid paths.
patterns.example: // service: input.EffectivePeriod = productcatalog.EffectivePeriod{} before calling adapter.UpdatePlan

patterns.name: Custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON on Plan for polymorphic RateCards
patterns.description: serializer.go implements Plan.MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON using alias types to avoid recursion. RateCards serialized with RateCardSerde type discriminator (flat_fee / usage_based). Any new field on Plan/Phase/RateCard must be reflected in the serializer.
patterns.example: json.Marshal(plan) // uses Plan.MarshalJSON which dispatches through rateCardAlias

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[Plan] for status/deletion guards
patterns.description: validators.go provides IsPlanDeleted(at time.Time) and HasPlanStatus(statuses...) as composable models.ValidatorFunc[Plan] used with Plan.ValidateWith().
patterns.example: if err := p.ValidateWith(plan.HasPlanStatus(productcatalog.PlanStatusDraft)); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Typed NotFoundError wrapping models.NewGenericNotFoundError
patterns.description: Always return plan.NewNotFoundError(NotFoundErrorParams{...}) from adapters. Use IsNotFound() for errors.As detection. Never surface raw Ent not-found errors.
patterns.example: return nil, plan.NewNotFoundError(plan.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, Key: key, Version: v})

patterns.name: Input types embed inputOptions and call models.AsValidationIssues
patterns.description: CreatePlanInput and UpdatePlanInput both embed inputOptions{IgnoreNonCriticalIssues} and use models.AsValidationIssues+WithSeverityOrHigher in Validate() for draft-mode relaxed validation.
patterns.example: issues, err := models.AsValidationIssues(errors.Join(errs...)); if i.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues { issues = issues.WithSeverityOrHigher(models.ErrorSeverityCritical) }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (ListPlans, CreatePlan, DeletePlan, GetPlan, UpdatePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan, NextPlan) and all input types with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: PublishPlanInput.Validate() uses a 30s jitter clock check via pkg/clock.Now() — not time.Now().

key_file_guides.role: Plan aggregate with Validate(), ValidateWith(), and AsProductCatalogPlan().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Addons field is *[]Addon — only populated on expand. Never assume non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Phase and PhaseManagedFields types; ValidatePhaseManagedFields() and ValidatePhase() validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PhaseManagedFields.Validate() asserts PlanID != '' — always populate PlanID when constructing a Phase in adapters.

key_file_guides.role: RateCard wrapping productcatalog.RateCard with PhaseID; custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON dispatches on type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New rate card types require a case in UnmarshalJSON and the serializer switch.

key_file_guides.role: Custom JSON marshaler/unmarshaler for Plan using alias types to avoid recursion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: serializer_test.go covers round-trip and error paths; any new field on Plan/Phase/RateCard must be added here.

key_file_guides.role: IsPlanDeleted and HasPlanStatus validator funcs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsPlanDeleted takes a time.Time argument (the check time); use clock.Now() at the call site.

anti_patterns: Setting EffectivePeriod via UpdatePlan — it must be zeroed in the service; only Publish/Archive alter it.
Skipping resolveFeatures/resolveTaxCodes before persisting rate cards in the service.
Returning raw entdb.IsNotFound — always wrap in plan.NewNotFoundError.
Publishing events outside a transaction.Run closure — DB write and event publish must be atomic.
Editing openmeter/ent/db/ generated files — always regenerate with make generate.

decisions.decision: EffectivePeriod changes are strictly gated to Publish/Archive operations
decisions.rationale: UpdatePlan explicitly zeroes EffectivePeriod to prevent callers from manipulating plan status through the update path; status transitions are explicit state-machine operations.

decisions.decision: Custom JSON serializer on Plan instead of generated oapi-codegen types
decisions.rationale: productcatalog.RateCard is a polymorphic interface; the serializer dispatches on the type discriminator to pick the correct concrete struct, which generated decoders cannot handle.

decisions.decision: Version auto-incremented in service layer, not settable by callers
decisions.rationale: Prevents callers from forging version numbers; the service computes the next version from existing versions at create/next time.

code_examples.scenario: Service mutation with transaction.Run wrapping adapter call and event publish

openmeter/productcatalog/planaddon.purpose: Domain package for plan-to-addon assignment (PlanAddon): defines the PlanAddon aggregate (linking plan.Plan and addon.Addon with quantity/phase constraints), typed errors, domain events, and the Service + Repository interfaces.

patterns.name: PlanAddon embeds productcatalog.PlanAddonMeta + full plan.Plan + addon.Addon
patterns.description: PlanAddon = NamespacedID + ManagedModel + PlanAddonMeta + Plan plan.Plan + Addon addon.Addon. AsProductCatalogPlanAddon() converts to the base productcatalog type. Always ensure Plan and Addon are fully populated before constructing.
patterns.example: type PlanAddon struct { models.NamespacedID; models.ManagedModel; productcatalog.PlanAddonMeta; Plan plan.Plan; Addon addon.Addon }

patterns.name: NotFoundError with PlanIDOrKey + AddonIDOrKey params
patterns.description: NotFoundErrorParams supports namespace, ID, PlanIDOrKey, and AddonIDOrKey fields. Use IsNotFound() for errors.As detection. Error message format: 'plan add-on assignment not found [namespace=... plan.idOrKey=... addon.idOrKey=...]'.
patterns.example: return nil, planaddon.NewNotFoundError(planaddon.NotFoundErrorParams{Namespace: ns, PlanIDOrKey: planKey, AddonIDOrKey: addonKey})

patterns.name: Domain events carry PlanAddon pointer + UserID from session
patterns.description: Three events: PlanAddonCreateEvent, PlanAddonUpdateEvent, PlanAddonDeleteEvent. Delete event Validate() asserts PlanAddon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must be applied before constructing the event.
patterns.example: event := planaddon.NewPlanAddonCreateEvent(ctx, created); s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event)

patterns.name: UpdatePlanAddonInput.Equal(PlanAddon) for idempotency detection
patterns.description: UpdatePlanAddonInput implements models.Equaler[PlanAddon]. Service can check if the patch produces any change before calling the adapter, enabling idempotent update handling.
patterns.example: if input.Equal(*existing) { return existing, nil } // skip adapter call

patterns.name: GetPlanAddonInput dual-lookup: assignment ID or plan+addon pair
patterns.description: If ID is empty, PlanIDOrKey and AddonIDOrKey must both be provided. Same dual-lookup pattern as plan.GetPlanInput. Adapters must handle both lookup paths.
patterns.example: if i.ID == "" { if i.PlanIDOrKey == "" || i.AddonIDOrKey == "" { return err } }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface (ListPlanAddons, CreatePlanAddon, DeletePlanAddon, GetPlanAddon, UpdatePlanAddon) and all input types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreatePlanAddonInput requires PlanID and AddonID non-empty; Get/Update/Delete support both ID-only and plan+addon-pair lookup.

key_file_guides.role: PlanAddon aggregate with Validate() and AsProductCatalogPlanAddon().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate() calls both Plan.Validate() and Addon.Validate(); ensure both are fully populated before constructing.

key_file_guides.role: NotFoundError and IsNotFound; wraps models.NewGenericNotFoundError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Error message format includes 'plan add-on assignment not found' — differs from plan/addon error messages.

key_file_guides.role: Three lifecycle events; subsystem is 'planaddon'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PlanAddonDeleteEvent.Validate() requires PlanAddon.DeletedAt != nil — soft-delete must precede event construction.

key_file_guides.role: Repository extends entutils.TxCreator; 5-method CRUD interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TxCreator is required for TransactingRepo in adapter layer.

key_file_guides.role: Test helpers AssertPlanAddonCreateInputEqual, AssertPlanAddonUpdateInputEqual, AssertPlanAddonEqual in the planaddon package (not testutils).
key_file_guides.watch_for: These are in the main package so they are importable in adapter/service tests without a separate testutils import.

anti_patterns: Checking plan/addon status (draft, active) inside the adapter — status validation belongs in the service layer.
Returning raw adapter-level errors without wrapping in planaddon.NewNotFoundError.
Calling adapter methods outside transaction.Run for write paths — risks partial writes if event publish fails.
Hard-deleting PlanAddon rows instead of soft-deleting via SetDeletedAt.
Skipping eager-load of Plan and Addon edges after create/update — PlanAddon.Plan and PlanAddon.Addon must always be fully populated.

decisions.decision: PlanAddon carries full plan.Plan and addon.Addon embedded rather than just IDs
decisions.rationale: Callers (subscription sync, billing) need the full plan and addon data without additional lookups; eager-loading at the adapter layer avoids N+1 queries.

decisions.decision: Both ID-based and plan+addon pair-based lookups supported in Get/Update/Delete inputs
decisions.rationale: HTTP handlers and internal code may have either the assignment ID or only the plan/addon identifiers; supporting both avoids forcing a separate Get before Update.

code_examples.scenario: Service write operation with transaction.Run, cross-entity status validation, and event publish

openmeter/productcatalog/subscription.purpose: Bridges the productcatalog plan model to the subscription domain: defines PlanSubscriptionService (Create, Migrate, Change), PlanInput (ref vs inline discriminator), and Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types that implement the subscription.Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces. All subscription DB writes go through subscriptionworkflow.Service, never directly through subscription.Service.

patterns.name: PlanInput dual-path: ref vs inline plan.CreatePlanInput
patterns.description: PlanInput holds either a *PlanRefInput (existing plan lookup by key/version) or *plan.CreatePlanInput (inline spec). Use FromRef/FromInput setters; AsRef()/AsInput() return nil for the unused path. Validate() returns error if both are nil.
patterns.example: var pi plansubscription.PlanInput; pi.FromRef(&PlanRefInput{Key: "pro", Version: lo.ToPtr(2)})

patterns.name: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implement subscription interfaces
patterns.description: plan.go defines Plan, Phase, RateCard wrappers that implement subscription.Plan, PlanPhase, PlanRateCard. Plan.GetPhases() computes cumulative StartAfter durations by accumulating phase durations in order — reordering Phases breaks timing.
patterns.example: p := &plansubscription.Plan{Plan: fetchedPlan.AsProductCatalogPlan(), Ref: &models.NamespacedID{ID: fetchedPlan.ID}}

patterns.name: PlanSubscriptionService delegates all persistence to WorkflowService
patterns.description: Service layer resolves and validates the plan, then calls subscriptionworkflow.Service.CreateFromPlan / ChangeToPlan / Migrate. No direct subscription.Repository or Ent calls allowed in this service.
patterns.example: return s.workflowService.CreateFromPlan(ctx, workflowInput, &adaptedPlan)

patterns.name: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase collapses earlier phases in place
patterns.description: When StartingPhase is set, all phases before the starting phase have their Duration zeroed (not deleted). Must be called on the plan before passing to WorkflowService when StartingPhase is specified.
patterns.example: // service/create.go: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase(plan, *request.StartingPhase)

patterns.name: Custom-plan discriminator sniff via marshal+unmarshal in HTTP handler
patterns.description: In the http/ sub-package, custom-plan detection uses marshal+unmarshal to discriminate plan type rather than oapi-codegen As* helpers, which succeed on wrong types and silently produce zero values.
patterns.example: // http/create.go: marshal body to JSON then unmarshal into plan-specific struct to detect type

key_file_guides.role: PlanSubscriptionService interface with Create, Migrate, Change; request types MigrateSubscriptionRequest, ChangeSubscriptionRequest, CreateSubscriptionRequest.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChangeSubscriptionRequest.PlanInput and StartingPhase must be set consistently; StartingPhase only applies when PlanInput.AsRef() is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types wrapping productcatalog types to implement subscription.Plan interfaces; Plan.GetPhases() accumulates StartAfter durations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Plan.GetPhases() accumulates StartAfter by summing phase durations in order — any reordering of Phases breaks subscription timing.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service directly for create/change/migrate — always route through PlanSubscriptionService or WorkflowService.
Calling plan.Service.GetPlan directly instead of the internal getPlanByVersion helper — the helper normalises NotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError.
Setting StartingPhase without calling zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase — phases before the start will not be collapsed.
Importing app/common from testutils — it must stay independent to avoid import cycles.
Adding persistence calls directly in service/service.go — all DB writes go through WorkflowService.

decisions.decision: PlanInput discriminator (ref vs inline) instead of separate Create methods
decisions.rationale: Callers need to subscribe to either an existing stored plan or a transient inline spec without the service needing separate entry points; the discriminator makes the choice explicit and validates that exactly one path is set.

decisions.decision: Plan/Phase/RateCard adapter types implement subscription interfaces rather than using productcatalog types directly
decisions.rationale: Subscription domain defines its own Plan/PlanPhase/PlanRateCard interfaces; the adapter types bridge productcatalog.Plan to those interfaces without circular imports between productcatalog and subscription packages.

code_examples.scenario: Resolving a plan by ref and delegating subscription creation to WorkflowService
